Ad Text:
Control Systems Engineer
Full Time Permanent Role
Adelaide Based Position with Hybrid working arrangement

This position contributes to the strategic direction of control systems engineering and plays a key role in shaping both the technical delivery and product evolution of the organisation's automation platform. You'll collaborate closely with customers, project teams, and product managers to ensure solutions are not only technically sound but aligned with real operational needs.

Responsibilities:

Drive continuous improvement in engineering quality, testing, and delivery practices.
Act as a customer advocate - engaging directly with clients to understand operational challenges and ensure solutions deliver tangible value.
Provide feedback and insights that help shape the product roadmap, influence design standards, and guide future control system capabilities.
Manage engineering resourcing and assignment across projects in coordination with Project Managers.
Provide effort estimates and technical input for project proposals and implementation plans.
Lead or support major commissioning activities, including Factory and Site Acceptance Testing (FAT/SAT).
Promote consistent use of engineering standards, templates, and automated build/deployment processes.
Identify and introduce emerging tools and technologies that enhance reliability and efficiency.
Adhere to prevailing ISMS policies and procedures while delivering the duties.

Experience:

Proven experience in Control Systems Engineering, ideally in a product-based or vendor environment.
Strong practical background in FAT, SAT, and commissioning for industrial automation projects.
Expertise in IEC 61131 programming, SQL, and Windows Server/network architectures.
Excellent understanding of WHS principles and a demonstrated safety-first mindset.
Experience working in Agile or continuous improvement-focused environments.
Experience engaging directly with customers and end-users, translating operational requirements into product or configuration improvements.
Industry experience in agribusiness or resources sectors highly regarded.
Demonstrated leadership ability to mentor, motivate, and develop team members.
Strong communication skills and ability to engage effectively with both engineers and senior stakeholders.
Commercial awareness - balancing technical quality, delivery pace, and customer satisfaction.

Apply Now or reach out to Ivan Aureus at 04•• ••• 152 for a chat.

We’re partnering with a leading consultancy to recruit an experienced Snowflake Engineer to join a growing Data & AI practice delivering large-scale, client-facing transformation programs across enterprise environments.

This is an opportunity to work with modern cloud and data technologies, helping clients build scalable, AI-ready data platforms while collaborating with high-performing engineering and architecture teams.

The Opportunity As a Snowflake Engineer, you will be responsible for designing, developing and optimising cloud-based data solutions using Snowflake and modern data engineering tools. You’ll work closely with clients, architects and delivery teams to build scalable data products, develop robust pipelines and support enterprise data transformation initiatives.

This role would suit someone who enjoys hands-on engineering work in a fast-paced consulting environment and is passionate about modern cloud data platforms.

Key Responsibilities
Design and build scalable data products and solutions on Snowflake
Develop and maintain ETL/ELT pipelines and workflows
Perform data modelling, mapping and transformation activities
Integrate Snowflake with cloud platforms including Azure, AWS or GCP
Work with modern data engineering tools such as dbt
Optimise Snowflake performance, storage and query efficiency
Collaborate with stakeholders, architects and engineering teams on solution delivery
Support best practices across data engineering, security and platform optimisation
Key Skills & Experience
Strong SQL skills with the ability to write complex queries for data extraction, transformation and analysis
Proficiency in at least one programming language such as Python, Java or Scala
Hands-on experience setting up and managing Snowflake environments
Strong understanding of Snowflake security and RBAC, Performance tuning and optimisation, Data warehousing best practices
Experience working with at least one major cloud platform: Azure, AWS, GCP
Minimum 5+ years’ experience in Data Engineering and Data Warehousing
Strong experience with ETL/ELT processes and related tooling
Strong communication skills and ability to work in client-facing environments
If you’re passionate about cloud data engineering and enjoy building modern, scalable data solutions, we’d love to hear from you.

Office-based – no WFH

About Cornerstone Health

Cornerstone Health operates 16 comprehensive medical centres, delivering services across general practice, dental, radiology and allied health. With more than 20 years’ experience in primary healthcare, we support leading clinicians in delivering quality care through modern systems, structures and culture. We are at the beginning of an exciting AI programme that will transform how we deliver care to over 2.3 million patients a year.

About the role

We’re looking for an experienced AI Platform and Solutions Engineer to own the design, build, and deployment of production-ready AI solutions across Cornerstone Health’s patient, clinical, and operational systems. This is the most critical hire in our AI strategy, you will be the primary technical owner of our AI programme, working directly with the CIO to turn our AI roadmap into working software.

This is not a research or prototyping role. You will be delivering production AI into real healthcare environments. You’ll operate at both a strategic and hands-on level, establishing reusable standards and uplifting internal capability while delivering measurable improvements in efficiency, experience, and access to care.

This is a fully office-based role located in North Sydney (above the station). Candidates must be willing and able to work on-site full-time.

What you’ll do

Design and build production-ready AI agents, LLM-driven workflows, and API-integrated solutions across patient, clinical, and operational systems

Define and implement AI architecture across Azure OpenAI and AWS Bedrock, including data pipelines, identity, security, and compliance controls appropriate for a regulated healthcare environment

Integrate AI into patient-facing platforms, including the Our Medical app, telehealth, call centre, and queue management

Implement governance and safety controls: PHI handling guardrails, audit logging, and human-in-the-loop workflows

Establish reusable patterns for prompt orchestration, agent frameworks, evaluation, and monitoring

Train developers and product teams on how to use AI safely and effectively; build internal standards and playbooks

Work closely with the development and infrastructure teams to deliver end-to-end solutions




What we’re looking for

Essential

Degree or Diploma in IT or related discipline

5+ years’ experience in software engineering, platform engineering, or solution delivery roles

Demonstrated experience designing, deploying, and operating production AI solutions, including LLMs, RAG architectures, and agents

Strong software engineering background, this role requires solid code, not just prompt engineering

Experience with LLM frameworks

Cloud platform experience, Azure strongly preferred (Azure OpenAI, Azure Document Intelligence, Azure API Management)

API-driven and event-driven architecture experience; REST APIs, identity and access (Entra ID, OAuth)

Healthcare or regulated environment experience is highly valued and will be given significant weight in the selection process

Highly regarded

Architecture experience: vector databases, embeddings, retrieval pipelines

Experience with real-time systems: queues, workflows, telehealth platforms

Familiarity with healthcare data standards and clinical systems such as Best Practice or similar

Understanding of PHI handling obligations, audit logging, and compliance in a health context

 

Why join us

Own the AI programme from day one, including architecture, delivery, and capability uplift

Work directly with the CIO on the Cornerstone Health AI roadmap

Permanent full-time role with genuine strategic scope

Purpose-driven culture with a genuine focus on patient outcomes

Conveniently located in North Sydney (above the train station)

Work that directly contributes to improving healthcare access for all Australians

Associate AI Engineer


Our Engineering Standards

Balance Speed and Quality

Engineers are expected to balance delivery speed with a strong commitment to quality, meeting agreed timelines while producing reliable, maintainable, and well-tested solutions. Sound judgment in making trade-offs between velocity and long-term sustainability is essential.

Collaborate Effectively

Engineering is collaborative by default. Team members are expected to contribute constructively in design discussions, reviews, and planning, communicate clearly about progress and risks, and support shared team outcomes in both hybrid and distributed environments.

Build and Maintain Systems

Engineers are responsible for building new capabilities while maintaining and improving existing systems. This includes designing scalable solutions, reducing technical debt, supporting operational stability, and contributing to continuous improvement.

Operate with Autonomy

A high degree of autonomy is expected. Given clear objectives, engineers should independently translate problems into actionable technical approaches, proactively identify improvements, and continuously expand relevant technical expertise.

Ownership and Accountability

Ownership is fundamental. Engineers are accountable for the quality, performance, and customer impact of their work from design through post-release support, and are expected to follow through on commitments.

AI-Enabled Engineering

AI is reshaping how software is built, and we are committed to leveraging it as a force multiplier for creativity, impact, and capability. Engineers are expected to confidently apply strong technical fundamentals while embracing AI tools and approaches to enhance productivity, problem-solving, and innovation. Curiosity, adaptability, and enthusiasm for integrating AI into meaningful product development are essential.

Contribute to Team Culture

Engineers contribute positively to a culture of professionalism, transparency, low bureaucracy, and mutual respect, strengthening team performance through authenticity, curiosity, and collaboration.

About the Role! 


Karbon is at the cutting edge of AI and data products, and this role puts you at the centre of that progress. You'll have a direct hand in shaping both our product and the processes that power it. The ideal candidate will be confident contributing to Karbon's AI models in a distributed production environment, and equally skilled at building bespoke AI solutions — automating workflows, surfacing insights, and creating real efficiencies for our users.

What you will own:

Designing AI systems - you know how to analyse problems and apply machine learning to solve them
Machine learning - you will be expected to develop a wide range of machine learning applications. 
Productionise AI - You will contribute to building end-to-end agentic solutions in our application
Model evaluation and selection - You look beyond the basic evaluation metrics and consider wider impacts.
Data management - Work with data engineers to build and maintain data pipelines
Collaboration - You can work in a cross-functional team with data engineers, analysts and full stack developers.
What Sets You Apart (Qualifications) 

1-3 years of experience developing and deploying AI/ML solutions
Experience working with LLMs (RAG, Chaining, MCP, etc) 
Proficiency in Python and relevant ML frameworks (Sklearn, Pytorch, Tensorflow, spaCy, etc.)
Good understanding of traditional machine learning techniques (linear/logistic regression, randomForest, GBM, etc.)
Familiarity with machine learning development lifecycles
A Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Statistics, or equivalent experience is needed (Masters or PhD advantageous).

It would be advantageous if you have:

Experience in agentic frameworks (ADK, LangGraph, Agent SDK etc.)
Previous MLOps experience
Experience developing and maintaining data pipelines (Snowflake, DBT, etc) 
Previous experience in backend software development (in particular C#)
Knowledge of deep learning architectures
Experience deploying machine learning models to complex production environments (Previous experience with Azure is advantageous)

Ideal for engineers who thrive in structured environments, complex domain systems, and enterprise-scale reliability challenges.

Our Core Technology Stack


We build modern, scalable software on a thoughtfully designed stack:

Frontend: TypeScript and JavaScript across Ember (today), React, and React Native.
Backend: .NET / C# (Web API, .NET Core) powering distributed services.
Data: SQL Server with performance and integrity at scale.
Cloud: Microsoft Azure.
Observability: Metrics, logging, alerting, and dashboards in Datadog — because we believe you can’t improve what you don’t measure.

Our architecture continues to evolve as we scale. We invest in event-driven systems, well-defined microservices, and containerized deployments (Azure Container Apps) to build resilient, decoupled, and high-performing software.

If you care about clean service boundaries, reliable systems, and shipping with confidence — you’ll feel right at home here.

Why work at Karbon?

Gain global experience across the USA, Australia, New Zealand, UK, Canada and the Philippines
4 weeks annual leave plus 5 extra "Karbon Days" off a year
Flexible working environment
Work with (and learn from) an experienced, high-performing team
Be part of a fast-growing company that firmly believes in promoting high performers from within
A collaborative, team-oriented culture that embraces diversity, invests in development, and provides consistent feedback
Generous parental leave

Karbon embraces diversity and inclusion, aligning with our values as a business. Research has shown that women and underrepresented groups are less likely to apply to jobs unless they meet every single criteria. If you've made it this far in the job description but your past experience doesn't perfectly align, we do encourage you to still apply. You could still be the right person for the role!

We recruit and reward people based on capability and performance. We don’t discriminate based on race, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, lifestyle, age, educational background, national origin, religion, physical or cognitive ability, and other diversity dimensions that may hinder inclusion in the organization.

Generally, if you are a good person, we want to talk to you. 😛

If there are any adjustments or accommodations that we can make to assist you during the recruitment process, and your journey at Karbon, contact us at peo•••••••••••@karbonhq.com for a confidential discussion.

At this time, we request that agency referrals are not submitted for this position. We appreciate your understanding and encourage direct applications from interested candidates. Thank you!

Lead systems architecture for sustainment software and ERP platforms

Own Microsoft Dynamics backend configuration, customisation and integration

Solve complex integration and process issues across enterprise systems

Deliver sustainment applications using Agile and DevSecOps (CI/CD) practices

Execute the Digital Transformation Roadmap for sustainment tools

Integrate ERP systems with Enterprise IT and engineering platforms

Provide technical leadership and mentoring across architecture and DevSecOps

 

About You

You are a senior systems architect or enterprise applications lead with strong experience in ERP systems, Microsoft Dynamics, backend configuration and system integration. You enjoy solving complex technical problems and staying hands-on while leading delivery.

Essential:

Strong experience in systems architecture and enterprise application integration

Hands-on experience configuring Microsoft Dynamics

On-premises installation of Business Central (BC), Customer Engagement (CE), and ERP systems

Senior Reliability Engineer

Location: Maroochydore, Sunshine Coast QLD

Salary: $160,000 – $180,000 base + superannuation

Employment type: Full-time, permanent

About HolisticAM:

HolisticAM is a reliability engineering and asset management consultancy based on the Sunshine Coast. We are growing the team to keep up with client demand. We work with Tier 1 mining, processing, and heavy industry clients across Australia and the Pacific.

What sets the firm apart is operator credibility and tooling nobody else in ANZ can offer:

Sole ANZ distributor for ReliaSoft (BlockSim, Weibull++, RGA, XFMEA, RBI, Lambda Predict)

Accredited provider of Apollo Root Cause Analysis training

Proprietary Reliability as a Service (RaaS) delivery framework, developed over decades of front-line maintenance and reliability leadership at sites including BHP, Alcoa, and MMG

Current and recent engagements include BHP, Whitehaven Coal, LXML Sepon, Glencore, Alcoa, JBS Foods. The work is technical, real, and visible to the executives funding it.

About the Role

Reporting to the Engineering Leader, the Senior Reliability Engineer leads technical delivery on complex project scopes, quality-assures the work of the Reliability Engineers, and mentors junior team members to accelerate their development.

The role operates with significant autonomy on technical matters and is expected to contribute to the evolution of HolisticAM’s engineering standards and methodologies. This is the first line of technical quality control before deliverables reach the Engineering Leader for final sign-off.

What success looks like in year one

Lead delivery on at least two major client engagements end to end, with deliverables that pass first review by the Engineering Leader

Bring at least two Reliability Engineers measurably up the Skill Competency Assessment framework

Contribute at least one substantive improvement to a HolisticAM methodology, template, or standard

Become Apollo Root Cause Analysis certified and ReliaSoft proficient (paid for by HolisticAM)

Key Accountabilities:

Lead engineering delivery on complex or multi-phase project scopes end to end. Manage technical quality, timing, and client-facing communication.

Review and quality-check deliverables produced by Reliability Engineers before they go to the Engineering Leader for sign-off. Feedback should build capability, not just correct errors.

Provide structured technical guidance and on-the-job development to Reliability Engineers. Contribute to their plans under the Skill Competency Assessment framework.

Actively contribute to the evolution of HolisticAM’s engineering methodology, templates, and standards in collaboration with the Engineering Leader.

Run assigned projects to plan and budget, including Project Execution Plan (PEP) development, stage-gate reporting, and KPI tracking.

Spot follow-on work during delivery and flag it to the Engineering Leader and the Business Development Manager.

Key Technical Competencies:

The Senior Reliability Engineer is expected to have demonstrable proficiency in the majority the following areas.

Asset Criticality Assessment.

Maintenance Strategy Optimisation (RCM / PMO / FMEA / FMECA).

Reliability Modelling.

RAM Analysis.

Life Cycle Cost Analysis (LCC).

Loss Accounting Analysis (Downtime, Asset Performance KPI’s, Bad Actors etc.).

Essential qualifications and experience:

Degree in Mechanical, Electrical, Maintenance, Reliability, or related engineering discipline

10+ years industry experience with at least 5 years in a reliability-focused role in an asset-intensive industrial or consulting environment

Track record managing technical projects through the full lifecycle, including the development and management of detailed Project Execution Plans

Workshop facilitation experience across mixed stakeholder groups, and presenting findings to client management

Strong technical writing. Able to produce client-ready reports without extensive revision

Desirable certifications and experience:

CMRP (Certified Maintenance and Reliability Professional)

Experience with reliability modelling software (ReliaSoft, Isograph, or equivalent)

Apollo Root Cause Analysis Trained (we will put you through this if you’re not)

Mentoring or coaching experience with junior engineers

Familiarity with AI-assisted data analysis and engineering tools

Sector experience in mining, utilities, food processing, or heavy industry

Location and travel

This role is based at our Maroochydore office. There is no remote option. We believe senior engineers develop faster in a co-located team and our clients expect direct accountability for delivery.

You will need to already live on the Sunshine Coast or be willing to relocate. We are not considering international applicants for this role.

Travel to client sites is part of the work and is scheduled around client operations and project demands. We work to manage it around your commitments where we can.

How to apply

Apply via Seek with your CV and a short cover note that tells us what you have actually delivered, not just what you have been responsible for. Applications close 15/06/2026.

Company description:



Deloitte Services Pty Ltd



Job description:


Work with cutting-edge AI and machine learning technologies
Flexible work arrangements - work in a way that suits you best
Be part of a collaborative team shaping the future of AI-enabled audit

Do you have a passion for AI, machine learning, and solving complex business problems with data? Are you looking for an opportunity where your work will have real impact across large-scale business environments?

We are seeking a talented AI Engineer to join Deloitte's Audit & Assurance Data & Digital team. In this role, you'll help deliver innovative AI solutions that support the vision of an AI-enabled audit, working across emerging technologies including Generative AI, LLMs, anomaly detection, clustering, and knowledge graphs. This is an exciting opportunity to contribute to high-impact projects while working alongside experienced professionals in a collaborative and forward-thinking environment.

What will your typical day look like?

In this role, you'll work closely with business managers, engineers, and AI specialists to design, develop, and deploy scalable AI and machine learning solutions that support core business processes and decision-making. You'll contribute across the full AI lifecycle, from understanding business problems and designing architectures through to production deployment, monitoring, and optimisation.
Day to day, you'll be building and refining AI models, supporting the development of GenAI and LLM-based solutions, and helping ensure systems are reliable, secure, and scalable. You'll also contribute to best practices in code quality, testing, and governance, while collaborating with stakeholders to ensure solutions align with business objectives and regulatory requirements.

About the team

Deloitte's Audit & Assurance business brings innovative solutions that elevate trust and create enduring value for clients. Through the use of advanced technologies, the Data & Digital team is helping shape the future of audit by embedding AI and data-driven insights into the audit process.
The team works on a range of cutting-edge initiatives spanning Generative AI, machine learning, context engineering, knowledge graphs, anomaly detection, and intelligent automation. It's a highly collaborative environment where continuous learning, innovation, and experimentation are encouraged.

Enough about us, let's talk about you.

You may have all or some of the following skills / experiences:

2-4+ years of industry experience designing, developing, and deploying machine learning or GenAI solutions
Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Data Science, Statistics, Physics, or a related quantitative discipline
Strong Python programming skills with experience using libraries such as NumPy, Pandas, Scikit-learn, TensorFlow, or PyTorch
Strong understanding of the end-to-end ML and LLM lifecycle
Experience working with LLMs, prompt engineering, embeddings, RAG architectures, vector stores, semantic search, or fine-tuning techniques
Understanding of AI safety, hallucination mitigation, prompt security, and privacy-preserving approaches
Experience developing production-ready, well-tested, and maintainable code
Exposure to MLOps or DevOps practices for deploying and managing models at scale on Azure, AWS, or GCP
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills with the ability to explain technical concepts clearly
A collaborative mindset with a passion for innovation, continuous learning, and emerging AI technologies

Why Deloitte?

At Deloitte, we focus our energy on interesting and impactful work. We're always learning, innovating and setting the standard; making a positive difference to our clients and our society. We put coaching at the heart of what we do, helping our people grow their careers in any direction - whether it be up, moving into something new, or even moving across the world.
We embrace diversity, equity and inclusion. We have a diverse collection of people from different backgrounds, with different experiences, gender identities, abilities and thinking styles. What binds us together is a shared commitment to value everyone's perspective and to cultivate inclusion; so that our work environment is a safe space we can all belong.
We value in-person connection with our clients and our colleagues. We offer several ways for you to work flexibly so that you can serve your clients, stay connected with your team, and manage your personal priorities.
We help you live and work well. To support your personal and professional life, we offer a range of perks and benefits, including retail discounts, wellbeing leave, paid volunteering days, twelve flexible working options, market-leading parental leave and return to work support package.

Next Steps

Sound like the sort of role for you? Apply now.

#LI-Hybrid

By applying for this job, you'll be assessed against the Deloitte Talent Standards. We've designed these standards so that you can grow in your career, and we can provide our clients with a consistent and exceptional Deloitte employee experience globally. The preferred candidate will be subject to background screening by Deloitte or by their external third-party provider.

Work with world leading technology business at the forefront of innovation.

We're a 'Family Friendly' certified workplace - we understand the often many and varied roles our team members need to play within their own unique family setting and actively support them.  

 

Our team feel Leidos is a great place to work. Learn more about our culture and benefits by visiting us here https://www.leidos.com/company/global/australia/careers  

 

Do Work That Matters 

Leidos Australia delivers IT and airborne solutions that protect and advance the Australian way of life.  Our 2000 local experts, backed by our global experience and network of partners, are working to solve the world's toughest challenges in government, intelligence, defence, aviation, border protection and health markets.  

 

Your New Role and Responsibilities  

As a Data Engineer, you will contribute to the delivery of enterprise geospatial dataflows and analytics pipelines supporting mission?critical outcomes within secure and complex environments.

 

Working under the guidance of senior engineers, you will apply sound data engineering and geospatial practices to build, maintain, and improve dataflows that ingest, transform, and deliver geospatial and spatiotemporal datasets. The role provides exposure to enterprise data engineering patterns, with geospatial data as the primary application domain, while developing breadth and depth across tooling, standards, and regulated delivery environments.

 

You will be a capable and developing practitioner, able to deliver assigned work packages with reliability and quality, while building toward greater independence and technical leadership.

 

Key responsibilities include:

Design, develop, and maintain geospatial data pipelines and workflows under senior guidance

Implement data ingestion, transformation, validation, and delivery for geospatial and spatiotemporal datasets

Develop and manage dataflows using tools such as Apache NiFi, FME, and SQL-based transformations

Support geospatial ETL processes, including schema management, data validation, and preparation

Troubleshoot dataflow issues and support integration with downstream systems, analytics, and reporting platforms

Ensure data quality, documentation, and continuous improvement while collaborating within teams and mentoring junior members

 

What You'll Bring to Make An Impact  

Experience in data engineering, data integration, or geospatial engineering roles with exposure to spatial data

Hands-on involvement in ETL/ELT pipelines, including data transformation and validation

Familiarity with data integration tools (e.g., Apache NiFi, FME) and SQL for data processing

Basic scripting or automation skills (e.g., Python, PowerShell)

Ability to follow engineering standards and work effectively within regulated environments

Strong communication, teamwork, and learning mindset, with exposure to data quality, governance, and collaboration tools (e.g., JIRA, Confluence)
 

Don't worry if you don't tick all the boxes - if you meet most of them, we encourage you to submit your application.  We're most interested in your strengths, what you want to learn and how far you want to go.  

 

This role does requires the successful applicant to be an Australian Citizen and hold an active TSPV Clearance, accompanied by the ability to be successful for a DISA.

 

Diverse Team Members, Shared Values and a Common Purpose 

Providing our customers with smarter solutions takes an incredible team with diversity of thought, experience and perspectives driving innovation. Inclusion is at the heart of our culture and is one of our core values. It's about creating a workplace where everyone can do important work, feels welcome, valued, and respected, and has equal access to opportunities to thrive. Paul Chase - Chief Executive, Leidos Australia. 

 

Leidos Australia is an equal opportunities organisation and is committed to creating a truly inclusive workplace. We welcome and encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islanders, culturally and linguistically diverse people, people with disabilities, veterans, neurodiverse people, and people of all genders, sexualities, and age groups.  

 

Our five Advocacy Groups (Women and Allies Network,?Young Professionals, Defence & Emergency Services, Action for Accessibility and Abilities and Pride+) provide an opportunity for team members to connect and collaborate on shared interests, and work to support and celebrate our diverse community. 

 

Next Steps 

To apply for this role, follow the links or apply via our Careers page.  

Recruitment process - 1 virtual interview or 1 face to face, a psychometric test & background checks. 

Applicants may also need to meet International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) requirements. In certain circumstances this can place limitations on persons who hold dual nationality, permanent residency or are former nationals of certain countries as per ITAR 126.1. 

We are committed to making our recruitment process accessible to all candidates. Please contact our Careers team car•••••••@au.leidos.com if you'd like to discuss any additional support during your application or throughout the recruitment process.

About Leidos

 

Leidos is an industry and technology leader serving government and commercial customers with smarter, more efficient digital and mission innovations. Headquartered in Reston, Virginia, with 47,000 global employees, Leidos reported annual revenues of approximately $16.7 billion for the fiscal year ended January 3, 2025. For more information, visit www.Leidos.com.

 

Pay and Benefits

 

Pay and benefits are fundamental to any career decision. That's why we craft compensation packages that reflect the importance of the work we do for our customers. Employment benefits include competitive compensation, Health and Wellness programs, Income Protection, Paid Leave and Retirement. More details are available at www.leidos.com/careers/pay-benefits.

 

Securing Your Data

 

Beware of fake employment opportunities using Leidos' name. Leidos will never ask you to provide payment-related information during any part of the employment application process (i.e., ask you for money), nor will Leidos ever advance money as part of the hiring process (i.e., send you a check or money order before doing any work). Further, Leidos will only communicate with you through emails that are generated by the Leidos.com automated system - never from free commercial services (e.g., Gmail, Yahoo, Hotmail) or via WhatsApp, Telegram, etc. If you received an email purporting to be from Leidos that asks for payment-related information or any other personal information (e.g., about you or your previous employer), and you are concerned about its legitimacy, please make us aware immediately by emailing us at Lei•••••••••••••••@leidos.com.

If you believe you are the victim of a scam, contact your local law enforcement and report the incident to the U.S. Federal Trade Commission.

 

Commitment to Non-Discrimination

 

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to sex, race, ethnicity, age, national origin, citizenship, religion, physical or mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, pregnancy, family structure, marital status, ancestry, domestic partner status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, veteran or military status, or any other basis prohibited by law. Leidos will also consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories consistent with relevant laws.

About us:

At Lockheed Martin Australia, we believe that when people are empowered, inspired, and supported, they can achieve extraordinary things. We embrace flexibility, so our people can be their best inside and outside of work. We champion inclusion and continuous learning, ensuring all voices are heard and valued. And we thrive on collaboration, turning challenges into opportunities for growth and innovation.  As a leader in national security, our innovative and diverse programs create a more secure future for Australia across space, air, land, sea, and cyber domains. Every day is a chance to solve the world’s most complex challenges and make a profound impact. Experience it here.

AIR6500 will provide a Joint Air Battle Management System capability for the Royal Australian Air Force. The system will provide multi-domain integration of air, electromagnetic, and missile defence battle management including deployable & fixed operations centres, communications, and both active & passive sensors. The AIR6500 Hardware Engineering Lead as a member in the System Engineering Integration Team (SEIT) has the responsibility for providing technical guidance and ensuring design integrity and consistency of the Hardware components across the program throughout the project lifecycle. Your primary responsibility is to oversee and lead the Hardware technical aspects of the project, ensuring that all design and development activities are completed to the highest standard and meet the required technical specifications.

Responsibilities

Working on the AIR6500 project the Hardware Engineering Lead will be responsible to shepherd the program product development teams through the entire hardware lifecycle – from early concept validation to final production and certification. 

Key Responsibilities

Program‑wide oversight of all AIR6500 hardware development teams across the project lifecycle.

Architectural leadership – drive the AIR6500 hardware solution and interfaces within the overarching program architecture.

Requirements & trade management – management of hardware and non‑functional requirements along with shaping the sell‑off strategy.

System integration – work closely with System Architects to ensure the correct sizeing and specification of hardware elements for AIR6500.

Metrics & performance monitoring – capture and assess key hardware metrics (on‑time releases, defect rates, supplier impact).

Subcontractor oversight – validate subcontractor analysis reports and ensure alignment with program specifications.

Mentorship & knowledge transfer – coach Lockheed Martin engineers, conduct cross‑skilling, and promote knowledge sharing.

Active hands‑on execution – roll up your sleeves to execute activities in addition to review and mentoring duties.

Ruggedized solution development – design deployable and ruggedized packaging hardware components.

Documentation & data packaging – Review and approval of technical documentation and Technical Data Packs.

Test & verification – review and approval test plans, procedures, and reports

 

About you:

Skills to be successful in this role you will bring:

Hardware Engineering Expertise - experience in hardware design, hardware architecture including ICT systems, thermal management, and ruggedization of hardware to meet environmental qualification including where the use of COTS equipment may be required.

Lifecycle Leadership - Proven track record leading hardware teams from concept to certification, with strong program‑management skills.

System‑Level Integration - Ability to translate system architecture requirements into tangible hardware solutions.

Metrics‑Driven - Comfortable using data to drive decisions on schedule, quality, and supplier performance.

Stakeholder Management - Skilled at influencing and negotiating with cross‑functional teams, suppliers, and customers.

Mentorship - Passion for coaching engineers and building high‑performing teams.

Hands‑on Engineering - adept at hands‑on design, prototyping, and testing—authentic “roll‑up‑the‑sleeves” attitude.

Documentation - excellent written communication, experienced with technical data preparation for certification.

Tools Proficiency – awareness and any experience in DOORS, 3D CAD systems such as CREO.

 How we reward and support you:

When you join Lockheed Martin, you work on projects you won’t experience anywhere else.

We offer you a range of benefits built to suit you, no matter what stage of life you are in. 

Flexible work & Hybrid work options

Access to our online discounts program offering immediate discounts or cashback

A variety of leave options including Purchase Leave to support you and your family  

Additional benefits such as Novated Leasing and Fitness Programs

Learning & Development platforms to encourage ongoing self-development

Special Requirements:

Applicants must be eligible to obtain and maintain an Australian Defence Security Clearance at NV1 and employment may be contingent on the provision of further personal information including citizenship to ensure compliance with international export control legislation and regulations.

A Veteran-friendly Workplace:

Veterans are encouraged to apply. We highly value the unique skills and experiences that veterans and transitioning service members can contribute to our team. We are proud to be recognised as a Veteran Friendly Employer by the Australian Government.

Equal Opportunity Statement:

Lockheed Martin Australia supports Equal Employment Opportunity and values diversity in the workplace by creating a place of employment that is, free of unlawful discrimination and is harassment free, fair, ethical, inclusive, and values the contribution of all its employees. By leveraging our employees’ unique talents and experiences, we will deliver innovative, affordable solutions and unparalleled customer value.

As part of our endeavours to increase participation of Indigenous Australians, we welcome applications from all people, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people.

We are committed to creating a workplace where everyone can thrive. If you are selected for this role, we would be happy to discuss any accommodations that will help you to be successful.

Senior AI Engineer – Enterprise AI Enablement
-Sydney
-$160,000 - $180,000 + Super + Bonus
  
Join one of the most forward-thinking teams in Sydney, working within a business that is genuinely AI-first in its approach. There is a defined strategy, real budget, and enterprise-wide commitment to embedding AI across products and operations.
  
We’ve recently partnered closely with the business to appoint their Engineering Manager, and are now supporting the next phase of the build-out. Off the back of that hire, we’re adding multiple engineers to deliver on a pipeline of greenfield AI initiatives.
  
The focus is clear: strong software engineering foundations, with exposure to AI-native environments. This is not a data science role, it’s about engineers who can build the systems and platforms that enable agentic AI at scale.
  
You’ll be working on production-grade systems across multiple use cases, from customer-facing agents and chatbots to internal AI capability, partnering closely with teams responsible for fine-tuning models. A solid understanding of agentic ecosystems and LLMOps will go a long way in helping you succeed.
  
Overview of the Role
You’ll be responsible for building and owning scalable backend systems that power enterprise AI solutions. This is a hands-on engineering role, focused on writing high-quality code, automating workflows, and creating the foundations required for AI to operate reliably in production.
Operating in a modern DevOps environment, you’ll take end-to-end ownership across design, build, deployment, and ongoing optimisation. You’ll work closely with data and AI teams to integrate models into real-world applications, ensuring they are performant, scalable, and maintainable.
  
Key Skills

5+ years software engineering experience in Agile/DevOps environments, ideally with Python (OOP)
Strong end-to-end SDLC experience across design, build, test, and production support
Proven ability to build scalable backend systems and write production-grade code
Experience with CI/CD pipelines and deployments in containerised environments (Docker/Kubernetes)
Exposure to agentic AI / LLM frameworks (e.g. RAG, MCP, Vertex AI, LangChain, LlamaIndex)
Understanding of LLMOps practices for deploying, monitoring, and scaling AI models
  
This is a strong opportunity for engineers who want to stay close to the tech, build meaningful systems, and play a key role in how AI is actually delivered across a large enterprise.

Senior AI-Augmented Software Engineer
About Leaselab

Leaselab is redefining novated leasing through technology, operational excellence, and customer experience. We build scalable systems that simplify salary packaging and vehicle finance while delivering exceptional outcomes for employers and drivers across Australia.

We operate in a fast-moving environment focused on innovation, automation, and continuous improvement, leveraging modern infrastructure and AI-assisted workflows to accelerate delivery and improve operational efficiency.




The Opportunity

We are seeking a highly capable Senior AI-Augmented Software Engineer to help design, build, and scale the next generation of Leaselab’s technology platform.

This role is ideal for an experienced engineer who uses AI as a force multiplier to rapidly build, review, and operationalise software systems without compromising engineering quality.

You will work across software engineering, system architecture, infrastructure, deployment workflows, and AI-assisted development, taking ownership of technical delivery from planning through to production.

This is a high-autonomy role suited to someone who can independently solve complex technical challenges while collaborating closely with business stakeholders to translate requirements into scalable production-ready systems.




Key Responsibilities
AI-Assisted Engineering

Build features and systems using AI-assisted development workflows including Cursor, copilots, agents, and LLM tooling

Review, validate, and productionise AI-generated code

Identify architectural, security, scalability, and logic issues within generated outputs

Create repeatable AI workflows that improve engineering velocity and consistency

Maintain high engineering quality while delivering rapidly

Technical Architecture & Systems Design

Design scalable backend systems and application architecture

Make informed technical decisions balancing speed, scalability, and maintainability

Structure systems to support reliable AI-assisted development practices

Evaluate and improve existing architecture, workflows, and operational processes

Ensure systems remain maintainable as complexity grows

Infrastructure & Deployment

Build and manage AWS infrastructure using SST and Infrastructure as Code principles

Own CI/CD pipelines and deployment workflows across multiple environments

Improve deployment reliability, rollback safety, and operational visibility

Implement monitoring, logging, and observability practices

Support secure, scalable, and resilient production infrastructure

Product & Technical Scoping

Translate business requirements into detailed technical scopes and implementation plans

Break down complex initiatives into actionable deliverables

Identify technical risks, edge cases, and operational considerations early

Collaborate with stakeholders to shape practical and scalable technical solutions

Produce clear technical documentation and implementation specifications

Engineering Quality & Ownership

Review pull requests and AI-generated code with strong attention to detail

Maintain high standards for code quality, testing, and platform consistency

Take ownership of features from planning through deployment and operational support

Improve engineering processes, tooling, and development workflows

Contribute to a culture of accountability, pragmatism, and continuous improvement




Skills & Experience
Essential Experience

Strong experience with TypeScript

Experience building and managing AWS cloud infrastructure

Strong knowledge of SST (Serverless Stack)

Experience with Infrastructure as Code

Backend and API development experience

CI/CD pipeline management and deployment workflows

Git workflows and code review practices

Automated testing frameworks and methodologies

Experience using AI-assisted development tooling in production environments

Desirable Experience

Experience with Cursor or similar AI-native development environments

Building agentic workflows or automation systems

Event-driven and serverless architectures

Monitoring and observability tooling

Async processing and distributed systems

Workflow optimisation and developer tooling




About You

You are a highly capable engineer who combines strong technical judgement with the ability to move quickly using AI-assisted workflows.

You are comfortable owning technical delivery end-to-end and thrive in environments where autonomy, problem-solving, and continuous improvement are valued.

You will also demonstrate:

Strong systems thinking and architectural capability

A pragmatic approach to balancing speed with engineering discipline

The ability to rapidly understand and improve existing systems

Strong communication skills with the ability to translate business requirements into technical execution

A proactive mindset focused on scalability, maintainability, and operational leverage




Why Join Leaselab?

Opportunity to shape and scale modern AI-assisted engineering practices

High-impact role with significant ownership and autonomy

Fast-moving technology environment focused on innovation and automation

Collaborative and pragmatic culture

Opportunity to work on meaningful systems that directly impact business growth and customer experience




Apply Now

Job Title: Platform Manager - Data & AI - Hawthorn East
Ad Text:
We’ve been trusted to serve Aussie communities since 1914 and grown to become a top 30-listed on the ASX with over 115,000 team members and a portfolio of iconic brands. At Coles Group, you’ll not only get to make a difference to millions of Aussie lives—you’ll also get to see your impact.

 

 
About the team

 

As the way Australians shop continues to evolve, our Data and AI Team keep Coles at the forefront of modern retail. We combine data, artificial intelligence, and applied engineering to reimagine how our stores, supply chain, and digital channels operate — creating smarter, more connected, and seamless experiences for our customers and team members.

 

Operating as a centralised development and enablement function within Coles Technology, the team unites product, engineering, and platform capabilities to deliver the intelligence layer of the enterprise. We serve every part of Coles — from computer vision applications that enhance in-store experiences and reduce loss, to forecasting, optimisation, and decision-support solutions that drive efficiency, accuracy, and agility across our supply chain, retail operations, and corporate functions.

 

The scale and impact of this work are significant. Coles serves millions of customers each week across more than a thousand stores nationwide, and our AI and data capabilities provide the intelligence that underpins how we plan, move, sell, and serve. By combining deep data capability with applied AI and modern engineering, we’re enabling Coles to become a truly AI-enabled enterprise, transforming how we operate and how Australia shops every day.



About the role

 

We are currently recruiting for a Platform Manager – AI/ML who will Lead the design, development, and delivery of AI platform capabilities focused on Generative AI, LLMs, and agentic systems, including model access layers, orchestration frameworks, prompt management, and tool integrations.

 

Key responsibilities of the role will include:

 

Drive the development of scalable, secure, and governed AI services that enable teams to build and deploy AI-powered applications and agents.
Collaborate with product, engineering, and business teams to translate use cases into reusable AI platform capabilities and patterns.
Establish and enforce best practices for AI governance, prompt engineering, evaluation frameworks, and responsible AI usage.
Build and evolve agent orchestration, tool integration layers, and model routing/gateway capabilities across multiple providers.

 

 

About you and your skills

 

Proven years in software/platform engineering, AI/ML, or related fields, with 3+ years in leadership roles.
Experience working with LLMs, GenAI platforms, or AI-driven applications in enterprise environments.
Strong understanding of:
LLM ecosystems (Azure OpenAI, OpenAI, etc.)
Prompt engineering & evaluation techniques
Agent frameworks and orchestration patterns
API design, microservices, and platform engineering
Experience with model integration, API gateways, and distributed systems.
Familiarity with AI governance, responsible AI, and risk management frameworks.
Experience managing cost, usage, and performance of AI services at scale.

 

 

Qualifications

 

Expertise in AI platform architecture, GenAI patterns, and scalable service design.
Strong understanding of agentic workflows, tool integration, and orchestration layers.
Ability to balance innovation with governance, control, and enterprise requirements.
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills.
Ability to translate evolving AI capabilities into practical, enterprise-ready solutions.

 

Education

 

Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, or related field.
Relevant certifications in cloud platforms, AI/ML engineering, or DevOps are a plus.

 

 

We are ideally seeking an experienced Platform Manager to drive the strategy, engineering, and operational excellence of scalable AI/ML platforms that enable teams to efficiently and securely build, deploy, and govern AI solutions, supported by strong people leadership and stakeholder management capabilities.



 
What’s in it for you?

 

Flexible working options: We know that work is only one part of your life, so we actively encourage a positive work-life balance and provide hybrid working options to help you achieve it.
Office perks: Take advantage of our gym facility and fitness classes, free parking, BBQ area, mini-Coles supermarket, fooderie hub where you can sample new products before they hit the shelves, school holiday program and so much more when you come in.
Discounts: Eligible team members receive 5% discount all year round on your Supermarket and Liquor online and in-store purchases. We also offer additional periods of double discount (10%) at various times throughout the year. As well as hotdeals exclusive to team members that translate into additional savings.
Reward through recognition: Give and receive recognition, linked to our Coles values, through our digital recognition platform ‘mythanks’. You can accumulate points to redeem in the online shop for exciting gifts and electronic gift cards from an extensive range of retailers.
Opportunities for learning and development: No matter where you start within our diverse business, you’ll have experiences, exposure, and education to satisfy you. Discover and explore a variety of career development programs and job-specific training.
Paid parental leave: We understand how important your life outside work is and offer permanent team members paid parental leave to support you in balancing work and family.

About the role




AGL has an exciting opportunity for a Reliability Engineer to join our Hydro Renewables team supporting 14 hydro power stations across Victoria and New South Wales.



Location: based in our Melbourne office or Mount Beauty office.



This role offers a unique balance of city-based flexibility and meaningful connection to some of Australia's most iconic renewable energy assets. While your home base will be Melbourne - providing access to a collaborative corporate environment, strong professional networks, and hybrid flexibility - you'll also regularly travel to Mount Beauty to stay closely connected to the operational teams and hydro assets you support.



You'll have the opportunity to spend time on-site within the Kiewa Valley, gaining firsthand exposure to major hydro infrastructure, complex engineering challenges, and the people who keep these assets operating safely and reliably. What you'll be doing


Data-driven reliability expertise, using turbine performance data, condition monitoring outputs, and failure trends to proactively improve asset reliability

Technical problem-solving capability, applying root cause analysis, FMECA, and practical, cost-effective monitoring solutions

Strategic maintenance planning experience, refining Reliability-Centred Maintenance strategies and optimising maintenance tasks, intervals, and spares based on asset criticality

Strong collaboration skills, working cross-functionally with engineering, maintenance, and operations teams to drive continuous improvement

Analytical and systems capability, including SAP and reliability tools, to support reporting, planning, and decision-making


What you'll bring


A Bachelor's degree in Engineering, ideally Electrical or Mechanical preferred OR experienced reliability professionals and trade-qualified technicians with demonstrated expertise in asset reliability, maintenance, and operational performance.

Experience applying Reliability Engineering techniques and Asset Management principles to improve performance and maintenance outcomes

Demonstrated experience leading reliability improvement initiatives within power generation or a similar heavy industry environment

Experience with SAP as a CMMS is highly desirable

Familiarity with platforms such as Power BI, OSI PI, RCA, reliability, and condition monitoring software is desirable

Ad Text:
At McNab, we recruit people who want more than just the average job. The people who thrive here are the ones who enjoy solving complex problems, improving the way businesses operate, and taking ownership of meaningful outcomes.

We’re building a modern data capability with strong backing from executive leadership, and we’re looking for a Senior Data Engineer who wants the freedom to help shape that journey.

About the Role

As a Senior Data Engineer at McNab, you’ll play a key role in designing and evolving our modern data platform across Azure, Snowflake and Airflow.

This role is more than building pipelines — we’re looking for someone who can think strategically, identify opportunities for improvement, influence technical direction, and proactively solve problems across the broader business.

You’ll work closely with the Data & Analytics Manager and wider teams to design scalable data solutions, improve engineering standards, and help drive innovation across our growing technology ecosystem. We’re particularly interested in people who enjoy experimenting with new tools, leveraging AI and automation, and continuously improving the way teams work.

This is an opportunity to join a business where technology investment is strongly supported from the top down, and where good ideas can quickly turn into action.

Key responsibilities include:

Assist in shaping and delivering McNab’s data engineering roadmap.
Design, build and maintain scalable data ingestion pipelines using Airflow.
Develop and optimise enterprise-grade data models and warehouse solutions.
Continuously improve pipeline performance, scalability, reliability and observability.
Build and maintain CI/CD processes using GitHub Actions.
Leverage LLMs and automation tooling to improve engineering workflows and code quality.
Support data governance, security and access management requirements across the business.
Collaborate closely with analytics, applications and business teams to deliver fit-for-purpose solutions.
Contribute to engineering standards, documentation and best practices across the team.

About You

You’re someone who enjoys ownership, thinks ahead, and doesn’t wait to be told where improvements can be made.

You’ll likely bring:

Demonstrated experience as a Data Engineer or in a similar role.
Strong experience building modern cloud-based data platforms.
The ability to work autonomously and confidently lead technical outcomes.
Strong communication skills and the ability to collaborate across technical and non-technical teams.
A proactive mindset with a genuine interest in solving business problems, not just technical tasks.
A passion for continuous improvement, innovation and emerging technologies.
Experience mentoring or positively influencing other team members through collaboration and leadership.

Technical Skills:

Advanced Python skills for data engineering and processing complex data structures.
Expert-level SQL skills including optimisation, modelling and security.
Strong experience with Snowflake, Airflow and modern cloud infrastructure.
Experience with Azure resources including Virtual Machines, Function Apps and Logic Apps.
Strong experience with Git, GitHub Actions and CI/CD principles.
Comfortable working within Linux/terminal-based environments.
Interest and practical exposure to AI-assisted development workflows and LLM tooling.

Desired Skills & Experience:

Experience working alongside Data Science, DevOps or Software Engineering teams.
Experience designing enterprise-scale data models and modern data architectures.
Exposure to analytics engineering or data application development environments.

About McNab

McNab is one of the largest Construction, Development and Property groups in Australia, with turnover now exceeding $1bn each year. Despite our growth and size, we've never lost our small company DNA - which centres around genuine care for our staff, customers and all our stakeholders. Success for us isn't about awards or growth, it's when we deliver so well that our customers are proud to wear a McNab T-Shirt on weekends!

We genuinely care. We make it easy. We deliver certainty.

This is our promise - to our clients, and everyone we deal with. Delivering on these three things really well is what drives us at McNab. Everything we do, from recruiting and training to performance and promotion, centres around this promise.

We leverage the entire McNab Group to deliver certainty for our clients at a time of unprecedented uncertainty in the industry. And our approach works. From delivering our own property developments, to manufacturing our own building products, and a plethora of aligned businesses, your career at McNab goes well beyond traditional construction roles.

Birthday leave – Celebrate your special day on us!
Bank and Employee Discounts - Access a range of exclusive savings and offers.
Social Club and team events – Work hard, play hard with regular team events.
Annual health and fitness subsidy – Stay fit and healthy with our health benefits
Fitness passport membership - Access to a wide range of gyms and fitness facilities
Parental leave program – Supporting you through life’s important milestones with tailored options to you and your family
Employee Assistance Program – We’ve got your back when you need it
Tailored development opportunities – We invest in your growth

This is an outstanding opportunity to join a business genuinely investing in technology and data, where you’ll have the opportunity to influence direction, implement new ideas and help shape the future capability of the organisation.

If this sounds like the kind of opportunity you’ve been looking for — APPLY NOW!

Please note: shortlisted applicants will be asked to complete a skills assessment.

The opportunity

The Boeing MQ28 - Ghost Bat is a globally-significant program that will deliver a disruptive advantage to support Defence operational capabilities. Developed in partnership with the Royal Australian Air Force, it is the first military combat aircraft to be designed, manufactured and assembled in Australia in 50 years.

Boeing Defence Australia (BDA) are seeking a Senior Systems Engineer to support the design, development integration and test of new capabilities within the MQ-28 Ghost Bat.

As a Senior Systems Engineer you will apply your extensive experience to a broad range of activities being undertaken by the Analysis Integration and Test (AIT) team to enhance the MQ-28 platform.

The role is based in Brisbane.

As an experienced Senior Systems Engineer you will:

Lead and develop requirements for the MQ-28 Mission System communication (datalink) and custodian interface.
Lead and development of design solutions to meet the capability objectives and requirements of the MQ-28 Mission System communication (datalink) and custodian interface.
Manage and define Mission System interfaces for communication (datalink) and custodian systems.
Lead and develop verification requirements, test plans, procedures and reports.
Coordinate, plan and conduct integration and formal verification testing of the MQ-28 Mission System
Support ground and flight test operations for the MQ-28 Mission System focusing on the MQ-28 Mission System communication (datalink) and custodian interface.
Lead and development of engineering documentation (Specifications, Design Documents, Interface Definitions, Test Procedures etc.).
Provide input to Mission System Technical Performance Measures.
Be the technical interface to the custodian development organisations external to BDA.

To be considered for the role you will have:

Minimum of 5 years' experience in complex technical development and integration projects. (essential)
Experience in military data links (MIL-STD-6016 - Link 16) and Universal Command & Control Interface (UCI). (desirable)
Hold tertiary qualifications in either Aerospace, Avionics, or Mechatronics, that satisfies the requirements for membership with Engineers Australia (MIEAust) or equivalent technical experience in a Systems Engineering discipline.
Demonstrated experience in systems and software development lifecycle (essential).
Demonstrated experience in avionics system level testing in a laboratory and ground test environments (desirable)
Experience with aircraft avionics and UAV systems (desirable).
Familiarity with engineering change processes.
Experience with Agile delivery methodologies (highly regarded)
Experience with Autonomous Systems projects (highly regarded)
Experience with aerospace and / or Defence projects (highly regarded.)

Applicants must be Australian Citizens to meet Defence security requirements.

You may require a Foreign Work Authorisation (FWA) in order to perform this role. This is not a requirement set or determined by Boeing but by the Department of Defence under laws issued in 2024. Please refer to the Department of Defence website to understand these requirements.

What's on offer?

Boeing values the health and wellbeing of our employees and offer true flexibility including working from home, compressed work weeks, and opportunities for part time arrangements, allowing you to balance your career with what is important to you outside of work.

Other benefits:

Work on cutting edge projects with opportunities to work across platforms.
Attractive remuneration and annual bonus.
Formal mentoring and training as well as on the job learning.
Formal reward and recognition program.
Access discounts for health insurance, travel and accommodation.
Paid study leave, paid parental leave and Defence leave.
Salary packaging options available.
Health and wellbeing benefits including annual flu vaccinations and Employee Assistance Program.
Social and community groups.

BDA works with strong links with our global Boeing community and we strongly encourage collaboration with our international counterparts.

If you are ready to join an innovative industry leader and would like to register your interest in working for Boeing, please click Apply Now.

Do Work That Matters 

Leidos Australia delivers IT and airborne solutions that protect and advance the Australian way of life.  Our 2000 local experts, backed by our global experience and network of partners, are working to solve the world’s toughest challenges in government, intelligence, defence, aviation, border protection and health markets.  




Your New Role and Responsibilities  

We have an opportunity for a senior Verification & Validation (V&V) Engineer to support testing and assurance activities across multiple mission systems operating within complex, multi‑domain security environments.




In this role, you will independently plan, execute, and document verification and validation activities for systems, components, and operational processes. You will work closely with technical teams, V&V leadership, and stakeholders to ensure delivered capabilities meet defined requirements, standards, and user expectations.




This role suits an experienced V&V practitioner who is confident operating with minimal supervision, contributing to test improvement initiatives, and supporting less experienced team members.




Key responsibilities include:

Plan and execute verification and validation activities across system changes, upgrades, and releases.

Develop and maintain test artefacts including test plans, cases, procedures, reports, and traceability.

Perform developmental, integration, and acceptance testing to ensure system quality and compliance.

Identify, analyse, and manage defects using test and defect management tools, supporting root cause analysis.

Collaborate with engineering and sustainment teams to ensure testability, quality outcomes, and compliance with security and change controls.

Contribute to continuous improvement of V&V processes and provide guidance to junior team members.




What You’ll Bring to Make An Impact  

Qualification in IT, Engineering, Computer Science, or equivalent practical experience.

Proven experience in verification and validation, systems testing, or quality assurance roles.

Experience developing and maintaining test artefacts aligned to requirements and standards.

Familiarity with test management and defect tracking tools (e.g. ALM, Jira, Confluence or similar).

Understanding of systems engineering principles and structured V&V practices.

Experience testing enterprise ICT environments, with exposure to SOE, infrastructure, applications, or test automation/scripting considered an advantage.




Don’t worry if you don’t tick all the boxes – if you meet most of them, we encourage you to submit your application.  We’re most interested in your strengths, what you want to learn and how far you want to go.
